introduction: A clinical syndrome resulting from bacterial blood stream infection with active proliferation of the organisms. Early-Onset sepsis manifests within the first 72 hours of life or at most 7 days of life while Late-Onset sepsis manifests after 7 days of life.
investigations: A lumbar tap for cerebrospinal fluid analysis is mandatory unless the infant has cardio-respiratory instability.
Neuro-imaging studies are required if fever persists or recurs, seizures recur after initial control, or occipitofrontal circumference is increasing.

non_drug: Fluid therapy with careful hydration management
Support care including fluid and caloric balance, blood transfusion for severe anaemia, oxygen therapy and ventilator support for hypoxaemia
drug: Intravenous ceftriaxone 50 mg/kg 12-hourly or cefotaxime 50 mg/kg 6-hourly and gentamicin 2.5 mg/kg 12-hourly
Intravenous ampicillin 50 mg/kg 6-hourly for Listeria monocytogenes infection
Phenobarbitone for seizures; 15 mg/kg loading dose and 2.5 mg/kg 12-hourly maintenance doses
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Fluid restriction not recommended for all infants due to dehydration risk and potential harm
